features.name: Industry-Wide Reporting & Analytics Gap — DFVA as Independent Analytics Standard
features.detail: Confirmed across 3 competitors (CourseLeaf, CourseLoop, Coursedog): no curriculum platform delivers adequate reporting/analytics. ListEdTech: 'These are data questions, not workflow questions.' DFVA's scoring dashboards fill the gap as an independent analytics layer.

features.name: Lightcast Data Freshness — Real-Time LMI Alternative
features.detail: Gartner reviews confirm Lightcast data is 'years behind' and 'could be outdated.' Combined with high pricing, this creates an opening for DFVA to use fresher, supplementary data sources (real-time job postings, AI-synthesized demand signals).

features.name: Market Convergence Validation — DFVA Thesis Confirmed by Third Independent Source
features.detail: ListEdTech, RFP.wiki, and InnoTechToday all confirm the market is converging toward data-centric 'academic operations platforms.' No vendor owns the analytics layer. DFVA's independent scoring standard has a validated market window — but it's closing as Coursedog ships LMI.

features.name: Structured Curriculum Data as Market Differentiator — Incumbents Store Degrees as Unstructured HTML
features.detail: GitHub coursedog-importer project (real university migration, 9 PRs Mar-Apr 2026) confirms both Coursedog and Modern Campus store degree requirements as freeform HTML blocks — NOT structured data. No curriculum platform in the market supports programmatic analysis of degree requirements. DFVA's structured assessment model is a fundamentally different data architecture, not just better analytics.

features.name: Data Portability as Competitive Weapon — Vendor Lock-in Confirmed in Curriculum Market
features.detail: Modern Campus provides ZERO data export on migration — university had to manually save HTML pages from Chrome before site decommissioned, then build scraper from scratch (6 weeks of work). Confirms vendor lock-in as deliberate strategy. DFVA's clean data import/export + independent positioning addresses this directly.

features.name: API Maturity as Competitive Differentiator — Coursedog Requires Browser Reverse-Engineering
features.detail: Coursedog API docs are inaccurate (password field naming), dual-ID system confuses developers, undocumented required parameters (doIntegration=true), and non-standard response formats. Developers must inspect browser network tabs to use the API. Clean, well-documented DFVA API would contrast sharply.

features.name: Second Coursedog Migration Confirms Universal Data Extraction Pain — DFVA Data Portability as Competitive Weapon
features.detail: Mercy University 25Live → Coursedog migration (FrankCoRa/Data_Migration_Project, June 27, 2026) is the SECOND confirmed Coursedog migration requiring custom data extraction code (first: Modern Campus → Coursedog, Mar-Apr 2026). Both needed Python/C# scraping because legacy systems provide zero structured data export. Pattern: every Coursedog migration requires custom automation. DFVA's independent scoring layer with clean data import/export avoids the migration trap entirely.

features.name: TechnologyOne Reporting Gap — Third-Party Analysis Tool Required, DFVA as Analytics Standard
features.detail: GitHub bianca-git/technologyone-analyser built a complete web app (TypeScript, IndexedDB, offline security) solely because T1's native reporting is insufficient. Parses T1's proprietary .t1etlp/.t1dm/.t1db/.t1xl formats. Very active: 7 issues closed June 7. Existence of a third-party analytics tool for T1 confirms the analytics gap thesis for DFVA's most relevant Go8 competitor. DFVA can position as the analytics standard T1 can't deliver.

features.name: Universal Curriculum Scraping — Structured Data as Product Category Differentiator
features.detail: GitHub rohan-c0de/cc-coursemap (1500+ issues, active June 28, 2026) scrapes Coursedog + CourseLeaf HTML catalogs. Issues show WAF battles, HTML sanitization pipelines, and manual degree-data wiring per college. Third independent confirmation (after coursedog-importer + T1 analyser) that curriculum data is unstructured HTML across all platforms. DFVA's structured assessment model — degrees as analyzable objects, not HTML — is a different product category.
